question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: For the patients who are diagnosed with SJS, how many of them have a normal level of total protein? diagnosed with SJS refers to Diagnosis = 'SJS'; normal level of total protein refers to TP > 6.0 and TP < 8.5;
orig_masked: For the [T3] who are [C61] with [V1], how many of them have a [C53]? [C61] with [V1] refers to [C61] = '[V1]'; [C53] refers to [C53] > [V2] and [C53] < [V3];; [V1] is a value of the column [C61]; [V2] is a value of the column [C53]; [V3] is a value of the column [C53]
orig_evidence: diagnosed with SJS refers to Diagnosis = 'SJS'; normal level of total protein refers to TP > 6.0 and TP < 8.5;
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.Diagnosis = 'SJS' AND T2.TP > 6.0 AND T2.TP < 8.5

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Who are the players that tend to be attacking when their mates were doing attack moves? List down their name. tend to be attacking when their mates were doing attack moves refers to attacking_work_rate = 'high';
orig_masked: Who are the [T4] that [C131]? List down their [C126]. [C131] refers to [C131] = '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C131]
orig_evidence: tend to be attacking when their mates were doing attack moves refers to attacking_work_rate = 'high';
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT t1.player_name FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE t2.attacking_work_rate = 'high'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Please list the names of the players whose volley score and dribbling score are over 70. volley score are over 70 refers to volleys > 70; dribbling score refers to dribbling are over 70 refers to dribbling > 70;
orig_masked: Please list the [C126] of the [T4] whose [C169] and [C138] are over [V1]. [C169] are over [V1] refers to [C169] > [V1]; [C138] refers to [C138] are over [V1] refers to [C138] > [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C169]
orig_evidence: volley score are over 70 refers to volleys > 70; dribbling score refers to dribbling are over 70 refers to dribbling > 70;
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT t1.player_name FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE t2.volleys > 70 AND t2.dribbling > 70

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What percentage of clients who opened their accounts in the district with an average salary of over 10000 are women? Female refers to gender = 'F'; Woman and female are closed; Average salary can be found in A11
orig_masked: What percentage of [T3] who [T3] their [C10] in the [T5] with an average [C18] of over [V1] are [C12]? [C12] refers to [C12] = '[V2]'; [C12] and [C12] are closed; Average [C18] can be found in [C18]; [V1] is a value of the column [C18]; [V2] is a value of the column [C12]
orig_evidence: Female refers to gender = 'F'; Woman and female are closed; Average salary can be found in A11
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(T2.gender = 'F') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.client_id) FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.A11 > 10000

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: How many gold-eyed superheroes did Marvel Comics publish?  gold-eyed refers to colour = 'Gold' where eye_colour_id = colour.id; superheroes that Marvel Comics published refers to publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics'
orig_masked: How many [V2]-[C19] [T9] did [V1] [T7]?  [V2]-[C19] refers to [C5] = '[V2]' where [C19] = [C6]; [T9] that [V1] [T7] refers to [C15] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C15]; [V2] is a value of the column [C5]
orig_evidence: gold-eyed refers to colour = 'Gold' where eye_colour_id = colour.id; superheroes that Marvel Comics published refers to publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN publisher AS T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.id INNER JOIN colour AS T3 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T3.id WHERE T2.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' AND T3.colour = 'Gold'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Among the superheroes with height from 170 to 190, list the names of the superheroes with no eye color.  height from 170 to 190 refers to height_cm BETWEEN 170 AND 190; no eye color refers to colour = 'No Colour'
orig_masked: Among the [T9] with [C23] from [V2] to [V3], list the [C28] of the [T9] with no [C19].  [C23] from [V2] to [V3] refers to [C23] BETWEEN [V2] AND [V3]; no [C19] refers to [C5] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C5]; [V2] is a value of the column [C23]; [V3] is a value of the column [C23]
orig_evidence: height from 170 to 190 refers to height_cm BETWEEN 170 AND 190; no eye color refers to colour = 'No Colour'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.superhero_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id WHERE T1.height_cm BETWEEN 170 AND 190 AND T2.colour = 'No Colour'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What is the average overall rating of the players born before the year 1986? average overall rating = DIVIDE(SUM(overall_rating), COUNT(id)); born before the year 1986 refers to strftime('%Y', birthday) < '1986';
orig_masked: What is the average [C153] of the [T4] [C121] [C121] the [C121] [V1]? average [C153] = DIVIDE(SUM([C153]), COUNT([C123])); [C121] [C121] the [C121] [V1] refers to strftime('%Y', [C121]) < '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C121]
orig_evidence: average overall rating = DIVIDE(SUM(overall_rating), COUNT(id)); born before the year 1986 refers to strftime('%Y', birthday) < '1986';
orig_gold_sql: SELECT SUM(t2.overall_rating) / COUNT(t1.id) FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE SUBSTR(t1.birthday, 1, 4) < '1986'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What is the average height of a non-human superhero in Dark Horse Comics? average = AVG(height_cm); non-human superhero refers to race <> 'Human'; Dark Horse Comics refers to publisher_name = 'Dark Horse Comics';
orig_masked: What is the average [C23] of a non-[V2] [T9] in [V1]? average = AVG([C23]); non-[V2] [T9] refers to [C17] <> '[V2]'; [V1] refers to [C15] = '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C15]; [V2] is a value of the column [C17]
orig_evidence: average = AVG(height_cm); non-human superhero refers to race <> 'Human'; Dark Horse Comics refers to publisher_name = 'Dark Horse Comics';
orig_gold_sql: SELECT AVG(T1.height_cm) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN publisher AS T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.id INNER JOIN race AS T3 ON T1.race_id = T3.id WHERE T2.publisher_name = 'Dark Horse Comics' AND T3.race != 'Human'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: How many male patients have a normal level of both albumin and total protein? male refers to Sex = 'M'; normal level of both albumin and total protein refers to ALB > 3.5 and ALB < 5.5 AND TP between 6.0 and 8.5;
orig_masked: How many [C64] [T3] have a [C53],[C14]? [C64] refers to [C64] = '[V5]'; [C53],[C14] refers to [C14] > [V1] and [C14] < [V2] AND [C53] between [V3] and [V4];; [V1] is a value of the column [C14]; [V2] is a value of the column [C14]; [V3] is a value of the column [C53]; [V4] is a value of the column [C53]; [V5] is a value of the column [C64]
orig_evidence: male refers to Sex = 'M'; normal level of both albumin and total protein refers to ALB > 3.5 and ALB < 5.5 AND TP between 6.0 and 8.5;
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.SEX = 'M'  AND T2.ALB > 3.5 AND T2.ALB < 5.5 AND T2.TP BETWEEN 6.0 AND 8.5

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Find the ratio between male superheroes and female superheroes.  ratio = DIVIDE(SUM(gender_id = 1) / SUM(gender_id = 2)); male superheroes refers to gender = 'Female'; female superheroes refers to gender = 'Male';
orig_masked: Find the ratio between [V2] [T9] and [V1] [T9].  ratio = DIVIDE(SUM([C21] = [V3]) / SUM([C21] = [V4])); [V2] [T9] refers to [C7] = '[V1]'; [V1] [T9] refers to [C7] = '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C7]; [V2] is a value of the column [C7]; [V3] is a value of the column [C21]; [V4] is a value of the column [C21]
orig_evidence: ratio = DIVIDE(SUM(gender_id = 1) / SUM(gender_id = 2)); male superheroes refers to gender = 'Female'; female superheroes refers to gender = 'Male';
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.gender = 'Male' THEN T1.id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) / COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.gender = 'Female' THEN T1.id ELSE NULL END) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN gender AS T2 ON T1.gender_id = T2.id
